findings.finding: Task 4 leaves the outer async handler's conflict-divert at outcome_router.py:1019-1022 unexamined/undocumented; the recoverable routing now depends on the inner _apply_feature_result re-deriving conflict=True. Consider routing the recoverable disposition at the outer layer where the first merge_result already exists.
findings.evidence_quote: Change shape: when merge_result.conflict is True, append {...recoverable_branch: actual_branch or None} to ctx.batch_result.features_deferred

findings.finding: The recoverable_branch contract guards only falsy actual_branch, not stale-but-truthy; research #7's assert-non-empty/has-commits mitigation was dropped. Safe today only because conflict implies a real non-empty branch (empty branch cannot conflict) — but that invariant is left implicit and would be too weak if the terminus broadened beyond conflict is True.
findings.evidence_quote: The recoverable_branch value MUST be actual_branch when truthy and MUST be None (never f"pipeline/{name}") when empty/absent.

findings.finding: Task 7 narrows the exec-summary deferred count to recoverable_branch is None but leaves the '(questions need answers)' label; the count stays divergent from render_deferred_questions's file-driven len(data.deferrals). Acceptance never asserts exec-summary/render_deferred_questions parity.
findings.evidence_quote: at report.py:367 change deferred = sum(... if f.status == deferred) to also require f.recoverable_branch is None

findings.finding: No task test exercises the live producer->carrier->consumer path; the consumers' contract (key off fs.recoverable_branch is not None) is verified only against fabricated state, never against the value Task 4 emits via _map_results_to_state.
findings.evidence_quote: Test (keyed recoverable_no_rebuild_followup): one recoverable feature + one genuine question-deferral
